attributes.gen_ai.operation.name: execute_tool
attributes.io.output:
1 --- 2 title: forbidden 3 description: API Reference for the forbidden function. 4 version: experimental 5 related: 6 links: 7 - app/api-reference/file-conventions/forbidden 8 --- 9 10 The `forbidden` function throws an error that renders a Next.js 403 error page. It's useful for handling authorization errors in your application. You can customize the UI using the [`forbidden.js` file](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/forbidden). 11 12 To start using `forbidden`, enable the experimental [`authInterrupts`](/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/authInterrupts) configuration option in your `next.config.js` file: 13 14 ```ts filename="next.config.ts" switcher 15 import type { NextConfig } from 'next' 16 17 const nextConfig: NextConfig = { 18 experimental: { 19 authInterrupts: true, 20 }, 21 } 22 23 export default nextConfig 24 ``` 25 26 ```js filename="next.config.js" switcher 27 module.exports = { 28 experimental: { 29 authInterrupts: true, 30 }, 31 } 32 ``` 33 34 `forbidden` can be invoked in [Server Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components), [Server Functions](/docs/app/getting-started/updating-data), and [Route Handlers](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route). 35 36 ```tsx filename="app/auth/page.tsx" switcher 37 import { verifySession } from '@/app/lib/dal' 38 import { forbidden } from 'next/navigation' 39 40 export default async function AdminPage() { 41 const session = await verifySession() 42 43 // Check if the user has the 'admin' role 44 if (session.role !== 'admin') { 45 forbidden() 46 } 47 48 // Render the admin page for authorized users 49 return <> 50 } 51 ``` 52 53 ```jsx filename="app/auth/page.js" switcher 54 import { verifySession } from '@/app/lib/dal' 55 import { forbidden } from 'next/navigation' 56 57 export default async function AdminPage() { 58 const session = await verifySession() 59 60 // Check if the user has the 'admin' role 61 if (session.role !== 'admin') { 62 forbidden() 63 } 64 65 // Render the admin page for authorized users 66 return <> 67 } 68 ``` 69 70 ## Good to know 71 72 - The `forbidden` function cannot be called in the [root layout](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/layout#root-layout). 73 74 ## Examples 75 76 ### Role-based route protection 77 78 You can use `forbidden` to restrict access to certain routes based on user roles. This ensures that users who are authenticated but lack the required permissions cannot access the route. 79 80 ```tsx filename="app/admin/page.tsx" switcher 81 import { verifySession } from '@/app/lib/dal' 82 import { forbidden } from 'next/navigation' 83 84 export default async function AdminPage() { 85 const session = await verifySession() 86 87 // Check if the user has the 'admin' role 88 if (session.role !== 'admin') { 89 forbidden() 90 } 91 92 // Render the admin page for authorized users 93 return ( 94
95

Admin Dashboard

117

120 ) 121 } 122 ``` 123 124 ### Mutations with Server Actions 125 126 When implementing mutations in Server Actions, you can use `forbidden` to only allow users with a specific role to update sensitive data. 127 128 ```ts filename="app/actions/update-role.ts" switcher 129 'use server' 130 131 import { verifySession } from '@/app/lib/d…(+1081)

1 --- 2 title: forbidden.js 3 description: API reference for the forbidden.js special file. 4 related: 5 links: 6 - app/api-reference/functions/forbidden 7 version: experimental 8 --- 9 10 The **forbidden** file is used to render UI when the [`forbidden`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/forbidden) function is invoked during authentication. Along with allowing you to customize the UI, Next.js will return a `403` status code. 11 12 ```tsx filename="app/forbidden.tsx" switcher 13 import Link from 'next/link' 14 15 export default function Forbidden() { 16 return ( 17
18

Forbidden

19

You are not authorized to access this resource.

20 Return Home 21
22 ) 23 } 24 ``` 25 26 ```jsx filename="app/forbidden.jsx" switcher 27 import Link from 'next/link' 28 29 export default function Forbidden() { 30 return ( 31
32

Forbidden

33

You are not authorized to access this resource.

34 Return Home 35
36 ) 37 } 38 ``` 39 40 ## Reference 41 42 ### Props 43 44 `forbidden.js` components do not accept any props. 45 46 ## Version History 47 48 | Version | Changes | 49 | --------- | -------------------------- | 50 | `v15.1.0` | `forbidden.js` introduced. | 51

It's useful for handling authorization errors in your application. You can customize the UI using the [`unauthorized.js` file](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/unauthorized). 11 12 To start using `unauthorized`, enable the experimental [`authInterrupts`](/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/authInterrupts) configuration option in your `next.config.js` file: 13 14 ```ts filename="next.config.ts" switcher 15 import type { NextConfig } from 'next' 16 17 const nextConfig: NextConfig = { 18 experimental: { 19 authInterrupts: true, 20 }, 21 } 22 23 export default nextConfig 24 ``` 25 26 ```js filename="next.config.js" switcher 27 module.exports = { 28 experimental: { 29 authInterrupts: true, 30 }, 31 } 32 ``` 33 34 `unauthorized` can be invoked in [Server Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components), [Server Functions](/docs/app/getting-started/updating-data), and [Route Handlers](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route). 35 36 ```tsx filename="app/dashboard/page.tsx" switcher 37 import { verifySession } from '@/app/lib/dal' 38 import { unauthorized } from 'next/navigation' 39 40 export default async function DashboardPage() { 41 const session = await verifySession() 42 43 if (!session) { 44 unauthorized() 45 } 46 47 // Render the dashboard for authenticated users 48 return ( 49
